sensors.name: L-Band Multi-Mission Radar
sensors.description: Lightweight counter-fire/counter-UAS radar. 360° rotating antenna provides persistent surveillance. Primary detection sensor for Group 1-3 UAS.

sensors.name: Ku-Band Fire Control Radar
sensors.description: Ku-band Radio Frequency Sensor. Precision fire-control radar that guides JACKAL interceptors to target. 360° surveillance with high-accuracy track data for engagement.
cons: Single target track at a time

sensors.name: EO/IR Camera
sensors.description: Pan/tilt/zoom electro-optical and infrared camera. 360° continuous rotation gimbal with thermal and daylight imaging. Used for visual identification of UAS targets.

effectors.name: RF/PNT Jammer
effectors.description: Radio frequency and Position/Navigation/Timing jammer. Disrupts UAS command links and GPS navigation. Effective against commercial drones.

effectors.name: DE-LASER-3km
effectors.description: Generic high-energy laser weapon system. Precision beam destroys Group 1-3 UAS by burning through airframe or electronics. Requires line of sight and clear atmospheric conditions. Engages one target at a time with sustained beam.
cons: Weather dependent (fog, dust, rain degrade beam)

effectors.name: DE-HPM-3km
effectors.description: Generic high-power microwave weapon system. Emits wide-area microwave pulse that disrupts UAS electronics and navigation systems. Effective against drone swarms — can engage multiple targets simultaneously with a single burst.
cons: Friendly electronics in beam path at risk
Does not physically destroy — drones may recover

effectors.name: JACKAL Pallet
effectors.description: Tube-launched interceptor guided by Ku-Band Fire Control Radar. Proximity-fused warhead. 4 interceptors per pallet. Requires Ku-Band FC Radar for mid-course guidance.
pros: High Pk against all UAS types

effectors.name: APKWS Launcher
effectors.description: Ground-launched laser-guided 70mm rocket launcher for precision C-UAS engagements. Effective against larger Group 2-3 UAS and one-way attack platforms when supported by a valid laser designation / target handoff. Limited magazine and elevated collateral/ROE considerations compared to non-kinetic systems.
pros: Good effect against fixed-wing and larger UAS
Useful against autonomous or RF-silent threats
Lower unit cost than interceptor missiles
cons: Requires line of sight and laser designation / target handoff
Collateral/ROE risk from kinetic intercept and debris
Less suitable for micro-UAS and dense swarms

combined.name: Shenobi
combined.description: Integrated RF detect-and-defeat system. Passive omnidirectional antennas scan 2.4GHz, 5.8GHz, 430MHz, and 900MHz bands for drone control links. Library-based — only detects drones with known RF signatures. Protocol Manipulation defeat sends targeted commands on the drone's own protocol: HOLD (freeze), LAND NOW (forced descent), DEAFEN (sever link). CM state progresses from 1/2 (downlink) to 2/2 (uplink acquired, full control).
cons: Defeat range (6km) shorter than detect range (8km)
